content.full_text:
Fern Polrrrcer- PRACTTcEs CorraMrssroN
428 J Street r Suite 620 o Sacramento, CA g5gl4_232g
(er6) 331660 
#tj1'6bd22_0886
Mr. Andrew Coolidge
Coolidge Public Relarions
P.O. Box 3231
Yuba City, California 95992
Re:
Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-09-243
Dear Mr. Coolidge:
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the campaign provisions
of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1 Because yotlr iltt., oik, g".r"rul q-uestions
about the Act, we have treated your request as one for informal ass"istance./ ih"
commission does not advise with respect to past conduct. (Regulation 1g32g(bxg)(A).)
Therefore, nothing in this letter should be construed to evaluate any conduct that may
have already taken place, and any conclusions contained herein ap;ly only to prospective
actions.
QUESTTONS
1' Under the Act, may you start to work as a political consultant for Butte County
District Attomey candidate Lance Daniel for two months, while you are still employed by
the political committee Citizens for Economic Balance which -uk", contribuiions
supporting Mr. Daniel? After the election, may you return to working only for the
committee Citizens for Economic Balance after having worked for canaiaate Lance
Daniel?
' The Political Reform Act is contained in Government code Sections g 1000 through g I 014. AII
statutory references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulationf of the Fair
Political Practices Commission are contained in Sections l8l l0 through lgggT of Title 2 of the California
Code of Regulations. All regulatory references are to Title 2, Division'6 of the california Code of
Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.
2 Inftrrmal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or
fbrmal written advice. (Section 831 l4; Regulation 1g329(cX3).)


File No. l-09-243
Page No. 2
2. May Citizens for Economic Balance pay for the time you spend working for
the candidate and report it as an in-kind donation to Lance Daniel's campaign?
3. Do any controlled committee issues arise if you provide consulting services for
both committees, Citizens for Economic Balance and Lance Daniel for District Attorney
2010?
CONCLUSIONS
1. Yes. Nothing in the Act would prohibit you from working as a political
consultant for the candidate Lance Daniel, while also still working for the political
committee Citizens for Economic Balance. In addition, nothing in ttre Aciwould prohibit
you from returning to working only for the committee Citizens for Economic Balance
after having worked for candidate Lance Daniel.
2. Yes. If Citizens for Economic Balance compensates you for the time you
spend working for candidate Daniel, these payments should be reported as in-kind
contributions to Lance Daniel's campaign. You state that Citizens for Economic Balance
is supporting candidate Daniel through contributions, not through any independent
expenditures, and also that there are no contribution limits in efiect in this iiutt" County
race.
3. You as a consultant will not be considered to control either committee, as the
Act's definition of "controlled committee" in Section 82016 only applies to a committee
controlled by a candidate. The analysis discusses when a committ#is considered to be
controlled by a candidate.
FACTS
You are a political consultant writing for advice on some campaign issues. When
we spoke by phone on November 12,2009, you provided additional facts that are
included here. You have for the last three months worked for a general purpose
committee, Citizens for Economic Balance. Citizens for Economic Balance is supporting
Lance Daniel, a candidate running against the incumbent in the Butte County race for
district attorney. Citizens for Economic Balance may change to a primarily formed
committee, given its support of Lance Daniel.
In addition, candidate Lance Daniel has a separate campaign committee, ,.Lance
Daniel for District Attomey 2010." Mr. Daniel would like to hire you as a consultant for
a period of two months to help develop his campaign for Butte County District Attorney.
You state that Citizens for Economic Balance is supporting candidate Daniel
through contributions, not through any independent efforts.-You ilso state that there are
no local contribution limits in effect in the Butte County district attorney's race.


File No. I-09-243
Page No. 3
With regard to your role as political consultant, you ask whether you may work
for both the candidate and the general purpose committee, and whether the general
purpose committee may compensate you for the time you spend working on t tr. Daniel,s
campaign and report this as an in-kind contribution to the cimpaign. yJu also ask
whether your role as a consultant to both committees gives rise to-any controlled
committee issues.
t. In-Kind contribut,r.r. 
":;::o 
campaigns can come in the form of
monetary donations, or donations of goods or services. Undei the Act, "nonmonetary,, or
"in-kind" contributions include goods and services provided free of charge to a candidate
or committee, and goods and services provided at a discount from the faii market value to
a candidate or committee (unless the same discount is given in the regular course of
business to members of the public). (Section 92015(cI Regulation rIzrsGX:).)
Volunteer personal services that an individual provides to a campaign are not'considered
a contribution. (Section 82015(9); Regulation 18215(c)(2).) Adonation of someone
else's paid services, however, is considered an in-kind contribution under the Act-3 For
example, if someone pays a consultant's fee for services provided to a campaign, this
constitutes an in-kind contribution in the amount of the payment.
Under the facts you have provided, Citizens for Economic Balance will be
coordinating its assistance in support of Lance Daniel's campaign with the candidate.
Citizens for Economic Balance is not planning to make indepen?ent expenditures on the
candidate's behalf. Under the Act, if a monetary or in-kind contributioir is ..made at the
behest of'or coordinated with the candidate, it must be reported as a contribution to the
candidate's campaign.
Therefore, if Citizens for Economic Balance compensates you for the time you
spend consulting for candidate Daniel's campaign, these payments will constitute in-kind
contributions from Citizens for Economic Balance to the candidate's committee, ..Lance
Daniel for District Attorney 2OlO." You state that there are no local contribution limits
applicable in the Butte County District Attorney race which would apply to these
payments.
3 Regulation 18423 describes when the "payment of salary, reimbursement for personal expenses,
or other compensation by an employer to an employee who spends more than loTo of his compensated time
in any one month rendering services for political purposes is i contribution . . . o, on.rp"rJitire . . .,' by
the employer under the Act. However, compensation paid to an employee by an employer who has
contracted to provide services to a candidate or committee are not contiiuutions o. "ip*aitu.es by the
employer, provided the services rendered by the employee are not beyond the scope oi'the contract.
(Regulation I 8423(e).)


File No. I-09-243
Page No.4
With respect to reporting, Citizens for Economic Balance will report making the
contribution to Lance Daniel on its Form 460, on Schedules D (summary of contributions
and expenditures supporting or opposing other candidates and measuresi and E
(payments made). Lance Daniel for District Attorney 2010 will report receiving the
contribution on Schedule C (nonmonetary contributions received). In addition, during
the l6-day late reporting period preceding the election, if more than one in-kind
contribution will be made or received from a single contributor, a candidate or committee
may file a single late contribution report covering the entire late contribution period
disclosing the total value of in-kind contributionJ that will be made or receivei from the
contributor during the period; or a good faith estimate of the value that will be
contributed or received du{ng the period. (Regulation tg4z5,copy enclosed.) The
candidate or committee will report the actual ,ralue of the in-kind contributions made or
received on its next campaign statement filed.
2. Controlled Committees. You asked by phone whether any controlled
committee issues would arise if you are providing consulting services for both
committees, Citizens for Economic Balance and Lance Daniel for District Attorney zol0.
For example, you asked whether you, the consultant, would be considered to control both
committees if you are working for both of them, or whether Citizens for Economic
Balance would be considered a controlled committee of the candidate.
The Act's definition of "controlled committee" only applies to a committee
controlled by a candidate. sectiong20L6 defines u...orrtrouJcommittee,, as:
"[A] committee which is controlled directly or indirectly by a
candidate or state measure proponent or that aits jointly with a candidate,
controlled committee, or state measure propore.ri in connection with the
making of expenditures. A candidate or state measure proponent controls a
committee if he or she, his or her agent, or any other committee he or she
controls has a significant influence on the actions or decisions of the
committee." (Section g2016)
Thus, Section 82016 describes two ways in which a committee may become a
controlled committee. First, by a candidate exerting significant influence on the actions
or decisions of the committee. Second, by acting jointly with a candidate in connection
with the making of expenditures. (Metlo AdviciLettei, No. 4-91-150; MullerAdvice
Letter, No. 4-89-305.)
To determine whether a candidate controls a committee under the first part of
Section 82016(a), one looks at the degree of the candidate's involvement in the
committee's activities. (Madden Advice Letter, No. A-85-197; KoppAdvice Letter, No.
A-97-108.) The Commission has interpreted the definition of "coniiolled committee,,
broadly to include any significant participation in the actions of a committee by a
candidate, his or her agent, or representatives of any other committee he or she controls.


File No. l-09-243
Page No. 5
(Higdon Advice Letter, No. I-94-189; KoppAdvice Letter, supra.) We have advised that
where a candidate is a voting member of an organization's leaaership, the candidate is
presumed to be the controlling candidate since the candidate exercisis significant
influence on the actions or decisions of the organizatio n. (Titus Advice Letter, No. I-06-
197; Trimb,zr Advice Letter, No. ,{-00-067: Gastelum Adviceletter, No. 4_96_l 13;
Ferguson Advice Letter, No. 4-86-044.) Other relevant factors which determine whether
a candidate is controlling a committee include whether the candidate is involved with
decisionmaking or developing or implementing campaign strategy for the committee.
(Helms Advice Letter, No. I-91-390.)
on the other hand, where a candidate is merely an honorary member of a
committee's governing body, who may appear in the ietterhead, but does not vote, make
decisions about committee funds, or otheiwise influence the actions of a committee, we
have advised that the candidate is not considered to "control" the comm ittee. (Lacy
Advice Letter, No. I-03-076; Erenbaum Adviceletter, No. I-01-242; MaddenAdvice
Letter, No. A-85-197.)
In your situation, Citizens for Economic Balance will not be considered to be
controlled by candidate Lance Daniel unless he has significant participation in the actions
of the committee or acts jointly with Citizens for Ecoiomic Balance in connection with
the making of expenditures. Whether a candidate is exercising control over a
committee's activities and expenditures is a factual question. You have not provided any
facts indicating that the candidate is controlling the activities or expenditures of Citizens
for Economic Balance. You have stated that hi is not on the board of that committee.
The only other fact you have discussed is that you are providing consulting services for
both committees. Section 82016 provides that a candidate controls a committee if ..he or
she, or his or her agent, or any other committee he or she controls has a significant
influence on the actions or decisions of the committee." Therefore, if a .uididut" *"r"
directing the activities or expenditures of a committee through his agent, ttre committee
would be considered to be controlled by the candidate. (TrimburAdvice Letter, supra;
Brainerd Advice Letter, Nos. I-90-413 and I-90-551.) ytu have not provided any facts
here, however, to indicate that the candidate is exercising control of Citizens for
Economic Balance, either directly, or through you as *-ug"nt.
3' General Purpose vs. Primarily Formed Committee. Lastly, you noted that
Citizens for Economic Balance may change from a general purpose committee to a
primarily formed committee, given its support of Lice Daniel. Under the Act, Citizens
for Economic Balance is considered a recipient committee under Section g1Ol3(a),
because the committee receives contributions totaling $1,000 or more in a calendar year
for political purposes.a The following definitions orlenerat purpose committee and
primarily formed committee set forth below will assiJt you in deiermining whether
Other types of committees are independent expenditure committees under Section g20l 3(b), and
major donor committees under Section AZO t :(t).


File No. I-09-243
Page No. 6
Citizens for Economic Balance will become a primarily formed committee because of its
support of candidate Lance Daniel.
Section 82027.5 of the Act defines the term "generar pulpose committee,, as
follows:
"(a) 'General purpose committee' means all committees pursuant to
subdivision (b) or (c) of section g2or3, and any committee pursuant to
subdivision (a) of Section 82013 which is formld or exists primarily to
support or oppose more than one candidate or ballot measure, e*""pt o,
provided in section 82047.5 [defining primarily formed committeej.
(b) A 'state general purpose committee' is a political party committee,
as defined in section g5205, or a corlmittee to support or oppose
candidates or measures voted on in a state election, or in more than one
county.
(c) A 'c-9r1nty general purpose committee, is a committee to support or
oppose candidates or measures voted on in only one county, or in m6re
than one jurisdiction within one county.
(d) A 'city general purpose committee' is a committee to support or
oppose candidates or measures voted on in only one city.,,
Interpreting mt statute, Regulation rgz47.s,copy enclosed, provides that a
general pu{pose committee will be considered a state, "iunty or cityiommittee based on
the jurisdiction where more than 50 percent of its campaign contributions and
expenditures are made. For example, if more than 50 p"rl"rt of a committee,s
contributions and expenditures are at the county level, it is a county committee.
The Act defines a "primarily formed committee" in Section g2O47.S as follows:
"'Primarily formed committee' means a committee pursuant to
subdivision (a) of Section 82013 which is formed or exiits primarily to
support or oppose any of the following:
(a) A single candidate.
(b) A single measure.
(c) A group of specific candidates being voted upon in the same city,
county, or multicounty election.
(d) Two or more measures being voted upon in the same city, county,
multicounty, or state election.,,
Regarding a "primarily formed committee,,' Regulatio n rg247.5(a) provides:
"(a) Primarily Formed committee. For purposes of Section g2047.5,
a recipient committee under section gzo]3d) is considered to be formed
or existing primarily to support or oppose a candidate or measure if it


File No. I-09-243
Page No. 7
(4) Two or more measures
multicounty, or state election."
makes more than 70 percent of its total contributions and expenditures, as
calculated pursuant to subdivision (c), on any of the following:
(1) A single candidate. A committee formed or existing primarily to
support or oppose a single candidate includes a committe" trrat makes
contributions and expenditures for a particular candidate and against that
candidate' s opponent(s).
(2) A single measure.
(3) A group of specific candidates being voted upon in the same city,
county, or multicounty election.
being voted upon in the same city, county,
-il{" ?, tda
By: Hyla P. Wagner
Senior Counsel, Legal Division
Thus, if Citizens for Economic Balance spends over 70 percent of its total
contributions and expenditures supporting Lance Daniel, or opposing his opponent, as
specified in Regulation 18247.5(c), the committee will be "orrid"."i 
u pri-u.ify formed
committee and its reporting obligations will change.
Enclosed is 1 coP{ of Campaign Manual 2 for Local candidates and committees
for your reference. It is also availablJ on the Fair Political practices Commissio;{*-"
website. If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel
Enclosures
HPwjgl

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: 1' Under the Act, may you start to work as a political consultant for Butte County
District Attomey candidate Lance Daniel for two months, while you are still employed by
the political committee Citizens for Economic Balance which -uk", contribuiions
supporting Mr. Daniel? After the election, may you return to working only for the

2. May Citizens for Economic Balance pay for the time you spend working for
the candidate and report it as an in-kind donation to Lance Daniel's campaign?
3. Do any controlled committee issues arise if you provide consulting services for
both committees, Citizens for Economic Balance and Lance Daniel for District Attorney
2010?

CONCLUSION: 1. Yes. Nothing in the Act would prohibit you from working as a political
consultant for the candidate Lance Daniel, while also still working for the political
committee Citizens for Economic Balance. In addition, nothing in ttre Aciwould prohibit
you from returning to working only for the committee Citizens for Economic Balance
after having worked for candidate Lance Daniel.
2. Yes. If Citizens for Economic Balance compensates you for the time you
spend working for candidate Daniel, these payments should be reported as in-kind
contributions to Lance Daniel's campaign. You state that Citizens for Economic Balance
is supporting candidate Daniel through contributions, not through any independent
expenditures, and also that there are no contribution limits in efiect in this iiutt" County
race.
3. You as a consultant will not be considered to control either committee, as the
Act's definition of "controlled committee" in Section 82016 only applies to a committee
controlled by a candidate. The analysis discusses when a committ#is considered to be
controlled by a candidate.
